title: IRGC Aerospace Commander Threatens 'Long and Painful Strikes' on US Regional Positions If Attacks Resume
summary: IRGC Aerospace Force Commander Majid Mousavi warned that any renewed US attack on Iran, even if limited, would trigger prolonged strikes on American military positions throughout the Gulf region, while Iran's new Supreme Leader pledged to maintain control of the Strait of Hormuz.
contents:
## IRGC Escalatory Warning

### Military Threat
A senior Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps official delivered a stark warning on May 1, declaring that any new American attack on Iran — even a limited one — would precipitate "long and painful strikes" targeting US regional positions. IRGC Aerospace Force Commander Majid Mousavi stated: "We've seen what happened to your regional bases — we will see the same thing happen to your warships."

### Supreme Leader's Message
Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei issued a written message to the Iranian people, declaring that Tehran would eliminate "the enemies' abuses of the waterway" under new management of the Strait of Hormuz. Khamenei, who has not appeared publicly since the February 28 strikes that killed his father and predecessor, pledged to protect Iran's "nuclear and missile capabilities."

### Air Defense Activity
Iranian semi-official Mehr news agency reported air defense activity in parts of Tehran late on Thursday, with the Tasnim news agency confirming that air defenses engaged small drones and unmanned surveillance aerial vehicles — suggesting heightened alertness to potential US or Israeli reconnaissance.

### Diplomatic Track
Despite the threatening rhetoric, Iran submitted a new negotiating proposal to Pakistani mediators on Thursday evening. The proposal reportedly seeks to defer discussion of Iran's nuclear program until after the conflict formally concludes — a condition Washington has rejected as a non-starter.

### Iranian Public Threats to Neighbors
The warnings extended beyond US targets. Bahrain's King Hamad condemned what he described as Iranian aggression against Manama, while the UAE banned citizens from traveling to Iran, Lebanon, and Iraq, signaling deep regional concern about potential Iranian retaliation against Gulf states that cooperated with the US-Israeli campaign.
